app.name: NWK
app.tagline: A safety net for travelers in Korea

home.headline: Before you exchange — real Korean prices.
home.kitLinkTitle: Korea kit — first 30 min, ATM, connectivity, emergency
home.pricesFootnote: Bucheon-si public data · 37 administrative dong avg · FX ₩1,330/USD

arex.title: AREX Airport Express
arex.detail: ICN → Seoul Station, 43 min · 06:30–22:50 · reserved seat

tmoney.title: T-money transit card
tmoney.detail: Buy & top up at any convenience store · works on subway, bus, some taxis

helpline.title: 1330 Tourist Hotline
helpline.detail: 24/7 · EN, JA, ZH, RU, VI · free translation, directions and complaints

atm.title: Airport & hotel ATMs
atm.body: ₩3,500–5,000 per withdrawal. CU/GS25 Global ATMs are around ₩1,000.

taxi.title: Short-trip taxi refusals
taxi.body: Under 3 km is often refused. Kakao T app locks the fare and prevents refusals.

guide.title: Fake guides in Myeongdong/Insadong
guide.body: "Free tour" approach → shop steering + tip demands. Official guides wear orange vests.

home.matchedTitle: Pick a destination. We match its prices, transit and rules.
home.sourceBody: KTO TourAPI · MOLIT VWorld · KMA · BOK. No ads, no commissions, no sponsors.

search.title: Find a place
search.subhead: Search tourist sites across Korea via Korea Tourism Organization's public data. Tap any result for the etiquette, pricing and safety tips that apply there.
search.mockNotice: Showing demo data. Live results will appear once a TourAPI key is configured.
search.emptyHint: Search by city, sight, or keyword

map.title: Map
map.subhead: Discover places and routes around you.

about.title: NWK — No Worries Korea
about.tagline: A safety net for travelers in Korea.

install.title: Add to home screen
install.body: Install NWK for instant launch and offline access to places you've recently viewed.

privacy.title: Privacy
privacy.body: Your location stays on your device and is never sent to our servers. Sign-in collects only your email and display name.

check.title: Let's check your travel bill
check.subhead: We'll compare it against Korea's real average prices in a few taps.

safeTransit.title: Korea's public transit is rip-off-free
safeTransit.body: Subway, bus, KTX and intercity bus all charge the listed price via T-money or cash. Only taxis need checking — always confirm the meter.

photo.title: Snap a menu or receipt
photo.comingSoon: Auto-detect items and prices from a photo — coming soon

form.taxiContext: Taxi is computed from distance and time

advice.careful: 15–40% above average. Worth checking a second place.
advice.bagaji: More than 40% over the fair range. You can report it to 1330 (24/7 multilingual).

check.sourceNote: Based on 2025 Seoul & Busan averages · Public data from Korea Consumer Agency

kit.title: Korea survival kit
kit.subhead: The numbers, hotlines and habits foreigners actually need from landing onward.

atmGlobal.title: CU/GS25 Global ATM
atmGlobal.body: Cheapest foreign-card withdrawals. Open 24/7 at every convenience store.

atmAirport.title: Airport & hotel ATMs
atmAirport.body: Convenient but 3–5× the fee. Use only in a pinch.

cardFirst.title: Card first, cash as backup
cardFirst.body: Almost every shop takes cards. Cash only needed in traditional markets.

exchangeMyeongdong.title: Myeongdong private booths
exchangeMyeongdong.body: 10–20 KRW/USD better than banks. Bring your passport.

esimKt.title: KT roaming eSIM
esimKt.body: 15 GB / 30 days. QR activation in under 10 minutes.

sim5day.title: 5-day unlimited SIM
sim5day.body: ICN arrivals floor, SKT/KT counters. Passport required.

wifiPublic.title: Public Wi-Fi
wifiPublic.body: Free on subway, buses and cafes. Look for 'Public WiFi Free' SSID.

profile.title: Me
profile.guestHint: Sign in to save favorites and history

price.water.title: Bottled water
price.water.body: 500ml at a convenience store. Tap water is drinkable but many locals still prefer bottled.

price.coffee.title: Cafe coffee
price.coffee.body: A regular Americano at a chain cafe. Specialty cafes run higher.

price.meal.title: Casual meal
price.meal.body: Average per-person meal at a local restaurant outside tourist zones.

price.taxi_base.title: Taxi base fare
price.taxi_base.body: Daytime base fare in Seoul. Late-night rides add roughly 20%.

price.subway_base.title: Subway base fare
price.subway_base.body: One ride with a T-money card. Cash adds about 100 KRW.

transit.tmoney.title: Get a T-money card
transit.tmoney.body: Rechargeable transit card sold at any convenience store. Works on subway, bus, and some taxis nationwide.

transit.subway_seoul.title: Seoul Metro
transit.subway_seoul.body: Nine numbered lines plus extensions. Most stations have English signage and free Wi-Fi.

transit.subway_busan.title: Busan Metro
transit.subway_busan.body: Four lines covering central Busan. T-money works here too.

transit.ktx.title: KTX high-speed rail
transit.ktx.body: Seoul to Busan in about 2h 40m. Book via the Korail app or at the station.

transit.taxi_app.title: Kakao T for taxis
transit.taxi_app.body: The most common taxi-hailing app. English interface and card payment supported.

etiquette.shoes_off.title: Remove shoes indoors
etiquette.shoes_off.body: Expected in hanok stays, temples, and many traditional restaurants. Look for a raised floor or shoe rack.

etiquette.bow.title: A slight bow is polite
etiquette.bow.body: A small nod when greeting or thanking someone is standard. Handshakes are fine too.

etiquette.no_tipping.title: No tipping culture
etiquette.no_tipping.body: Service charge is included. Cash tips can confuse staff.

etiquette.palace_respect.title: Palace grounds
etiquette.palace_respect.body: Stay on marked paths, no drones, and keep voices low inside preserved halls.

safety.emergency.title: Emergency numbers
safety.emergency.body: 112 police, 119 fire and ambulance. English support is available 24/7.

safety.tap_water.title: Tap water is safe
safety.tap_water.body: Seoul tap water meets WHO standards, though many travelers still prefer bottled.

safety.crowds.title: Watch for crowds
safety.crowds.body: Markets and shopping streets get dense on weekends. Keep bags in front of you.
